# Expanso Examples

> Source-backed tutorials and pipeline patterns for Expanso Edge. Use these examples to learn, adapt, validate, and deploy Expanso pipeline jobs.

The site contains interactive explorers, step-by-step tutorials, setup notes, troubleshooting guidance, and YAML stored in the public examples repository. Example scope varies: some files are teaching stages or configuration fragments, while files named `*-complete.yaml` are structured as complete job specifications. Check each example's setup page and source before use; external services, credentials, paths, and environment variables may be required.

## Start Here

- [Examples home](https://examples.expanso.io/): Browse the published examples and learning paths.
- [Local development setup](https://examples.expanso.io/getting-started/local-development/): Configure supporting services used by repository examples.
- [Expanso Edge installation](https://docs.expanso.io/getting-started/installation): Install Edge and the CLI on supported platforms.
- [Expanso CLI reference](https://docs.expanso.io/references/cli/expanso-cli/): Current commands for profiles, jobs, nodes, and executions.
- [Expanso Edge CLI reference](https://docs.expanso.io/references/cli/expanso-edge/): Current commands for bootstrapping and running Edge.
- [Component reference](https://docs.expanso.io/components): Inputs, processors, outputs, caches, rate limits, and other pipeline components.
- [Examples source repository](https://github.com/expanso-io/examples.expanso.io): Published source, YAML, validation scripts, and contribution history.

## Current Command-Line Workflow

For Linux or macOS, the official installers use separate endpoints for Edge and the CLI:

```bash
curl -fsSL https://get.expanso.io/edge/install.sh | bash
curl -fsSL https://get.expanso.io/cli/install.sh | bash

expanso-edge version
expanso-cli version
```

Clone the source repository and validate a complete job locally:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/expanso-io/examples.expanso.io.git
cd examples.expanso.io

expanso-cli job validate examples/getting-started/quickstart-complete.yaml --offline
```

Deploying requires an authenticated Expanso CLI profile and a reachable control plane. After reviewing the job's paths, services, credentials, and node targeting, deploy with the `job` command:

```bash
expanso-cli job deploy examples/getting-started/quickstart-complete.yaml
```

Use `expanso-cli job validate` before deployment and consult the CLI reference for profile and monitoring commands.

## Job Specification Shape

Complete deployable examples use a job wrapper around the pipeline configuration:

```yaml
name: example-job
type: pipeline
description: What this job does
namespace: default
labels:
  category: example

# Replace this value with a label present on the target node.
selector:
  match_labels:
    environment: replace-with-node-label

config:
  input:
    # one input component
  pipeline:
    processors:
      # zero or more processors
  output:
    # one output component
```

The showcased quickstart is intentionally minimal and uses a sample file path plus a placeholder node selector. Create the sample input or replace the path, and replace the selector value with a label on the node that should run the job before deployment.

Other tutorial stage files may contain only part of this structure. Prefer a matching `*-complete.yaml` file when validating or deploying a full job, then adapt its components and environment-specific values.

## Core Learning Paths

- [Log severity filtering](https://examples.expanso.io/log-processing/filter-severity/): Parse logs, filter by severity, and route selected records.
- [Structured log parsing](https://examples.expanso.io/data-transformation/parse-logs/): Explore parsing patterns for several log formats.
- [Content-based routing](https://examples.expanso.io/data-routing/content-routing/): Route records using conditional pipeline logic.
- [PII removal](https://examples.expanso.io/data-security/remove-pii/): Explore deletion, hashing, pseudonymization, and generalization patterns.
- [Enterprise migration examples](https://examples.expanso.io/enterprise-migration/): Review staged migration pipeline walkthroughs.
- [Splunk edge-processing walkthrough](https://examples.expanso.io/integrations/splunk-edge-processing/): Independent technical example for preparing and routing data to Splunk HEC.

## Usage Boundaries

- Treat examples as starting points, not universal production configurations.
- Review the setup and troubleshooting pages for the selected example.
- Confirm component support against the current component reference.
- Validate complete job files offline before connecting to a control plane.
- Test with representative non-production data and provide required credentials through documented secret mechanisms.

## Support

- [Expanso documentation](https://docs.expanso.io/)
- [Expanso Cloud](https://cloud.expanso.io/)
- [Support](mailto:support@expanso.io)
